buckets.title: Buckets
buckets.noBuckets: No buckets yet. Create one to get started.
buckets.bucketTypeRssNetworkDescription: Combine multiple RSS channels under one RSS network bucket.
buckets.bucketTypeRssChannelDescription: Connect Metaboost to an RSS podcast, music, or livestream feed.
buckets.bucketTypeCustomDescription: Create a custom MetaBoost bucket without RSS integration. You can nest up to three levels.
buckets.bucketTypeMbMidDescription: Add a second-level custom bucket under this root.
buckets.bucketTypeMbLeafDescription: Add a third-level custom bucket under this mid-level bucket.
buckets.endpointTabDescription: POST boost and stream payloads to this HTTPS URL using a valid AppAssertion. The request body follows the mb-v1 OpenAPI schema.
buckets.addToRssDescription: Use this tab to confirm your RSS feed URL and verification status before adding the MetaBoost value tag to your feed.
buckets.addToRssInstructions: Paste this metaBoost tag inside your RSS channel element, and then use the verify button to confirm it's setup correctly.
buckets.rssItemOrphanedWarning: Orphaned item bucket (item removed from latest feed parse)
buckets.name: Name
buckets.publicTooltip: Public buckets: all messages visible to everyone. Non-public: only you and bucket admins can see messages.
buckets.messagePublicTooltip: This message will be visible to everyone, not just the recipient.
buckets.messageBodyMaxLengthInvalid: Enter a whole number between 140 and 2500
buckets.minimumMessageUsdCentsInvalid: Enter a whole number between 0 and 2147483647
buckets.minimumMessageUsdCentsHelp: Threshold uses USD cents at message creation time (1 = $0.01, 100 = $1.00).
buckets.derivedBucketNameNotice: Name is derived from RSS metadata for channel and item buckets.
buckets.applySettingsScopePrompt: Apply these setting changes only to this bucket, or to all sub-buckets too?
buckets.rssItemsEmptyNeedsVerification: No RSS item buckets yet. To generate item buckets from this feed, open Add to RSS, add the podcast:metaBoost tag to your RSS channel, then run Verify Metaboost Enabled.
buckets.cannotEditBucketOwnerAdmin: You cannot edit the admin settings for the owner of a bucket.
buckets.blockedSendersDescription: Boost senders listed here are blocked from sending and excluded from message lists for this bucket and every sub-bucket.
buckets.blockedAppsDescription: Apps are allowed by default when registered. Uncheck to block an app for this bucket and all descendants.
buckets.blockedAppsGlobalDisabledTooltip: This app is blocked everywhere on this server.
buckets.blockedAppsRegistryDisabledTooltip: This app is suspended or revoked in the app registry and is blocked everywhere on this server.
buckets.settingsDeleteIntro: Permanently removes this bucket and everything inside it, including nested buckets and messages. You cannot undo this action.
buckets.settingsDeleteFailed: Could not delete this bucket. Try again.

settingsDeleteConfirm.message: Are you sure you want to delete {name}? All nested buckets and their data will be permanently removed. This cannot be undone.

buckets.bucketPermissionsInfo: Bucket permissions apply to this bucket and nested buckets.
buckets.addAdminDescription: Select the permissions you want to assign to the new admin. Pressing "Add admin" creates a link you can send to them; when they open it, they can accept or decline the invitation.
buckets.inviteLinkCopy: Share this link. When they open it, they can accept or decline.
buckets.roleDescriptionBucketFull: Grants full create, read, update, and delete permissions for buckets, messages, and admins.
buckets.roleDescriptionBucketRead: Grants read-only permissions for buckets and messages.
buckets.roleDescriptionCustomRole: Use a custom role when you need permissions that do not match a predefined option.

howToCreators.title: How-To for Creators
howToCreators.intro: Listener boost messages arrive through the mbrss-v1 standard. These steps are the usual setup in Metaboost.
howToCreators.step1Body: Create an RSS Network bucket for your show, then add RSS Channel buckets under it for each feed you want to connect.
howToCreators.step2Body: On a channel bucket, open the Add to RSS tab, paste the podcast:metaBoost snippet into your RSS channel, then run Verify Metaboost Enabled so Metaboost can confirm the tag is live.
howToCreators.step3Body: Choose whether the bucket is public (messages can appear on public URLs) or limited to you and admins. Share bucket links as needed. Ordinary boost messages appear in message views; other telemetry may be stored but not listed there.
howToCreators.policyMiddle: for payment and refund disclaimers. Developer integration details are in

howToDevelopers.title: How-To for Developers
howToDevelopers.intro: Integrate mbrss-v1 so your app can POST boost metadata after payments. Use the OpenAPI specs linked at the bottom for full request and response shapes.
howToDevelopers.step1Body: GET the mbrss-v1 capability URL referenced by the podcast:metaBoost tag. Apply message_char_limit, terms_of_service_url, schema_definition_url, and any public_messages_url the server returns.
howToDevelopers.step2Body: POST to the mbrss-v1 boost URL with the required fields. After split payments run, send exactly one POST once the largest split recipient’s payment has succeeded (same ordering as the mbrss-v1 contract). Use action boost for rows that appear in message lists; use action stream for telemetry that public message endpoints omit. Treat HTTP success as metadata storage, not proof that every recipient settled.
howToDevelopers.step3Title: 3) Read public messages and handle errors
howToDevelopers.step3Body: When public visibility is enabled, use the public message endpoints for bucket, channel, or item scope. Render amounts and units per schema; handle validation and rate limits without aggressive retries.
howToDevelopers.policyMiddle: for payment/refund disclaimers and responsibilities between sender, creator, and payment service provider. Creator onboarding guidance is in
